license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.imucc6w
title: TruSeq RNA Preparation with Modification for Four Rounds of Poly-A Selection
author: Dr. Daniel Richter

steps_raw.instruction: Start with 50 µL total RNA
Vortex RNA Purification Beads (RPB) to resuspend Let RNA Purification Beads stand at RT for 30 minutes before using
Add 50 µL RPB to RNA
Pre-heat thermal cycler lid to 100ºC
Run the mRNA Denaturation program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Remove PCR tubes when the cycler reaches 4ºC
Incubate tubes at room temperature (RT) on the bench for 5 min 00:05:00 This allows the RNA to bind to the beads
Place tubes on a magnetic stand at RT for 5 min This allows seperation of the poly-A RNA beads from the solution
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.
Remove tubes from the magnetic stand
Wash beads with Bead Washing Buffer (BWB) undetermined
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Place tubes on a magnetic stand at RT for 5 min 00:05:00
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube. Take care not to disturb the beads. The supernatant contains the majority of the ribosomal and other non-messenger RNA
Remove tubes from the magnetic stand
Add Elution Buffer (ELB) to each tube undetermined
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler lid to 100ºC
Run the mRNA Elution 1 program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler when it reaches 25ºC
Place the tubes on the benchtop at RT
Add Bead Binding Buffer (BBB) to each tube undetermined This allows mRNA to specifically rebind the beads, while reducing the amoung of rRNA that non-specifically binds.
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Place the tubes on a magnetic stand at RT for 5 min 00:05:00
Remove and discard supernatant from each tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
Wash the beads with Bead Washing Buffer (BWB) undetermined
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Place the tubes on a magnetic stand for 5 min 00:05:00

Remove and discard all of the supernatant from each tube. Take care not to disturb the beads. The supernatant contains residual rRNA and other contaminants, and were released in the first elution and did not rebind the beads.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
If this is the first time through the protocol: continue to repeat Poly-A Selection.

If this is the second time through the protocol: add Elute, Prime, Fragment Mix (EPF) to each tube undetermined The Elute, Prime, Fragment Mix contains random hexamers for RT priming and serves as the first strand cDNA synthesis reaction buffer.

Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly The Elute, Prime, Fragment Mix contains random hexamers for RT priming and serves as the first strand cDNA synthesis reaction buffer. After adding 18 uL Elute, Prime, Fragment Mix, continue to Incubate RFP .
Add Elution Buffer (ELB) to each tube undetermined
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler to 80ºC with the program 80ºC Program details can be found in the guidelines
Incubate the tubes for 2 min at 80ºC 00:02:00
Immediately transfer the tubes to the magnetic stand This prevents mRNA from re-binding to the beads
Transfer supernatant to a new set of tubes immediately after the beads have aggregated on the magnetic stand undetermined

Repeat the entire protocol from step 2 (Purify and Fragment mRNA - Make RBP / Vortex RNA Purification Beads to resuspend) to step 35 (Purify and Fragment mRNA - Make RFP / Add Elute, Prime, Fragment Mix)
Pre-heat the thermal cycler lid to 100ºC This is the step from which to continue after the second time through the Poly-A Selection protocol.
Run the program Elution 2 - Frag - Prime Program details can be found in the guidelines
Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler when it reaches 4ºC and centrifuge briefly Proceed immediately to Synthesize First Stand cDNA
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min 00:05:00
Transfer supernatant (fragmented and primed mRNA) to new tubes undetermined

Add First Strand Master Mix (FSM)/SuperScript II to each tube undetermined First Strand Master Mix (FSM)/SuperScript II is stored at -20C, thawed at RT Second Strand Master Mix (SSM) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Return the First Strand Master Mix/SuperScript II tube back to -20ºC immediately after use
Pre-heat the thermal cycler lid to 100ºC
Run the first strand program Program details can be found in the guidelines
When the thermal cycler reaches 4ºC, remove the tubes and proceed immediately to Synthesize Second Strand cDNA.
Add thawed Second Strand Master Mix (SSM) to each tube undetermined Second Strand Master Mix (SSM) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler to 16ºC with the second strand program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Incubate the tubes at 16ºC for 1 hr 01:00:00
Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler and let stand to bring to RT
Vortex the AMPure XP beads until they are well dispersed AMPure XP beads are stored at 4C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Add 90 µL beads to each tube containing 50 µL ds cDNA
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 15 min 00:15:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min 00:05:00
Remove and discard 135 µL supernatant from each tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.
With the tubes remaining on the magnetic stand, add 80% EtOH to each tube without disturbing the beads. undetermined
Incubate tubes at RT for 30 sec 00:00:30
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube without disturbing the beads
Repeat steps 66-68 for a total of two 80% EtOH washes
Let the tubes stand at RT for 15 min to dry 00:15:00
Remove them from the magnetic stand
Add Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to each tube undetermined
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min 00:05:00
Transfer 60 µL supernatant (ds cDNA) to a new set of tubes This is a safe stopping point for storage at -20 degrees C
Add End Repair Mix (ERP) to each tube undetermined End Repair Mix (ERP) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Adjust the pipette to 100 µL and gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler to 30ºC with the End Repair program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Incubate the tubes at 30ºC for 30 min 00:30:00
Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler
Vortex the AMPure XP beads until they are well dispersed AMPure XP beads are stored at 4C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Add 160 µL beads to each tube containing 100 µL of sample
Adjust the pipette to 200 µL and gently pipette up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 15 min 00:15:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until liquid appears clear
Remove and discard 127.5 µL of the supernatant from each tube
Repeat step 87 once
With the tubes remaining on the magnetic stand, add 80% freshly prepared EtOH to each tube without disturbing the beads undetermined
Incubate the tubes at RT for 30 sec 00:00:30
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube without disturbing the beads
Repeat steps 89-91 once, for a total of two 80% EtOH washes
Let the tubes stand at RT for 15 min to dry 00:15:00
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
Resuspend the dried pellet in each tube in Resuspension Buffer (RSB) undetermined Resuspension Buffer (RSB) is stored at -20 C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min, or until the liquid appears clear. 00:05:00
Transfer 17.5 µL of the clear supernatant to a new set of tubes This is a safe stopping point for storage at -20 degrees C
Add A-Trailing Mix (ATL) to each tube undetermined A-Tailing Mix (ATL) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Adjust the pipette to 30 µL and gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler to 37ºC with the A Tail program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Incubate the tubes at 37ºC for 30 min 00:30:00
Immediately remove the tubes from the thermal cycler, and proceed immediately to Ligate Adapters
Immediately before use, remove the Ligation Mix (LIG) tube from -20ºC storage.
Add Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to each tube undetermined Resuspension Buffer (RSB) is stored at -20 C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Add Ligation Mix (LIG) to each tube undetermined Return the LIG back to -20 C immediately after use
Add appropriate RNA Adapter Index to each tube undetermined RNA Adapter Indexes are stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Adjust the pipette to 37.5 µL and gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler to 30ºC with the Ligate Adapters program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Incubate the tubes at 30ºC for 10 min 00:10:00
Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler
Add Stop Ligation Buffer (STL) to each tube undetermined This step inactivates ligation Stop Ligation Buffer (STL) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT
Adjust the pipette to 42.5 µL and pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Vortex the AMPure XP beads until they are well dispersed AMPure XP beads are stored at 4C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Add beads to each tube undetermined
Adjust the pipette to 85 µL, then gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly.
Incubate the tubes at RT for 15 min 00:15:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Remove and discard 79.5 µL of the supernatant from each tube
With the tubes remaining on the magnetic stand, add freshly prepared 80% EtOH to each tube without disturbing the beads. undetermined
Incubate the tubes at RT for 30 sec 00:00:30
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube without disturbing the beads
Repeat steps 121-123 for a total of two 80% EtOH washes
Let the tubes stand at RT for 15 min to dry 00:15:00
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
Resuspend the dried pellet in each tube in Resuspension Buffer (RSB) undetermined Resuspension Buffer (RSB) is stored at -20 C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Transfer 50 µL of the clear supernatant to a new set of tubes
Vortex the AMPure XP beads until they are well dispersed
Add beads to each tube for a second clean up undetermined
Adjust the pipette to 100 µL, then gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly.
Incubate the tubes at RT for 15 min 00:15:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Remove and discard 95 µL of the supernatant from each tube
With the tubes remaining on the magnetic stand, add freshly prepared 80% EtOH to each tube without disturbing the beads. undetermined
Incubate the tubes at RT for 30 sec 00:00:30
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube without disturbing the beads
Repeat steps 138-140 for a total of two 80% EtOH washes
Let the tubes stand at RT for 15 min to dry 00:15:00
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
Resuspend the dried pellet in each tube in RSB undetermined
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Transfer 20 µL of the clear supernatant to a new set of tubes This is a safe stopping point for storage at -20 C
Add thawed PCR Primer Cocktail (PPC) to each tube undetermined PCR Primer Cocktail (PPC) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT, kept on ice
Add thawed PCR Master Mix (PMM) to each tube undetermined PCR Master Mix (PMM) is stored at -20 C, thawed at RT, kept on ice
Adjust the pipette to 50 µL then gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Pre-heat the thermal cycler lid to 100ºC
Run the PCR program Program details can be found in the guidelines
Vortex the AMPure XP beads until they are well dispersed AMPure XP beads are stored at 4C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Add beads to each tube undetermined
Adjust the pipette to 100 µL, then gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly.
Incubate the tubes at RT for 15 min 00:15:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Remove and discard 95 µL of the supernatant from each tube
With the tubes remaining on the magnetic stand, add 80% freshly prepared EtOH to each tube without disturbing the beads undetermined
Incubate the tubes at RT for 30 sec 00:00:30
Remove and discard the supernatant from each tube without disturbing the beads
Repeat steps 160-162 for a total of two 80% EtOH washes
Let the tubes stand at RT for 15 min to dry 00:15:00
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
Resuspend the dried pellet in each tube in Resuspension Buffer (RSB) undetermined Resuspension Buffer (RSB) is stored at -20 C, let stand at RT for 30 minutes before use
Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Place the tubes on the magnetic stand at RT for 5 min or until the liquid appears clear 00:05:00
Transfer 50 µL of the clear supernatant to a new set of tubes
Repeat steps 154-170 once. During the repeated steps, modify step 166 to add 31 ul Resuspension Buffer (RSB) and step 170 to transfer 30 ul to separate 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes.
Take one aliquot for Qubit and one aliquot for qPCR in 1.5 mL tubes undetermined
Freeze cleaned PCR and both aliquot tubes at -20ºC

Make Qubit working solution in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube by mixing 7 µL Qubit dsDNA HS reagent (the fluorescent label) with 1393 µL Qubit dsDNA HS buffer, and mix well. undetermined, enough for 5 libraries and 2 standards
Prepare standard 1 and standard 2 by mixing 10 µL of each standard with 190 µL working solution in seperate Qubit assay tubes
Add 1 µL of each library to 199 µL working solution in seperate Qubit assay tubes (1:200 dilution)
Mix all tubes by vortexing for 2-3 sec
Incubate tubes at RT for 2 min 00:02:00
Read standards 1 and 2 on the Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer
Read all library samples on the fluorometer and record their concentration (in ng/mL)

Dilute 1 µL aliquots of each library in seperate tubes to 300 pg/µL Determine the appropriate dilution to achieve this concentration by dividing the Qubit concentration by 1.5. For example, if the Qubit concentration is 152 ng/mL, then the library should be diluted at 1:101 (=152/1.5) for 300 pg/uL.

Allow the gel-dye mix to equilibriate to RT for 30 min before use 00:30:00 If gel-dye mix is not already prepared, allow the HS DNA dye concentrate (blue) and the HS DNA gel matrix (red) to equilibrate to RT for 30 minutes, add 15 uL of HS DNA dye concentrate to a HS DNA gel matrix vial, vortex well, spin down and transfer to a spin filter. Centrifuge at 2240 xg +/- 20% for 10 minutes and discard the filter.
Clean the Bioanalyzer electrodes by pipetting 350 µL nuclease-free water into a cleaning chip and placing it into the machine
Ensure that the chip priming station syringe clip is at the lowest position, that the base plate is in position C, and that the plunger is positioned at 1 mL.
Pipette gel-dye mix in the well at the third row and last column undetermined
Pop bubbles with a 30 gauge needle
Put the chip on the priming station and close it firmly until it locks into position
Press the plunger until it is held by the clip, wait exactly 1 minute, and release the clip. 00:01:00
Wait for at least 5 sec and slowly pull back the plunger to the 1 mL position
Open the priming station and remove the chip
Pipette gel dye mix in the remaining 3 wells of the last column undetermined
Pipette marker (green) in all sample and ladder wells undetermined
Pipette HS DNA ladder (yellow) in the well marked with a ladder (third column, last row) undetermined
Pipette diluted 300 pg/µL library aliquot into wells 1-11 undetermined
Add marker (green) into each unused well undetermined
Move to the chip vortexer and vortex for 1 min at 2400 rpm 00:01:00
While vortexing, remove the cleaning chip and leave the Bioanalyzer open to dry the electrodes
Run the chip in the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer within 5 min (to avoid evaporation).
Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly
Incubate the tubes at room temperature for 5 minutes 00:05:00
